title: cordyline indivisa
intro: Cordyline indivisa, also known as the cabbage tree or ti kouka, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive red or yellow flowers and large, cabbage-like leaves. The plant is said to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin problems due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of cordyline indivisa include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. Cordyline indivisa can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat topical skin issues and wounds.
